file_insights.scope: enum variant definition and single match arm in main function

patterns_discovered.description: When renaming enum variants, both enum definition and all pattern matches must be updated together to maintain compilation
Enum variant with associated struct field (shell) - maintains named field binding in match expression
patterns_discovered.significance: Field binding syntax unchanged by rename

patterns_discovered.description: Command enum used as dispatch mechanism in async main function with match statement routing

gotchas_discovered.description: If only enum definition renamed without updating match arms, compiler catches with 'unreachable pattern' or 'non-exhaustive patterns' error
Enum variant names must be searched globally - particularly important with generic names like 'Completions' which could appear in comments or documentation

approach_outcome.semantic_impact: renames variant to shorter, more idiomatic form
approach_outcome.breaking_changes: API-breaking for external code using full enum path, but appears internal to CLI crate

recommendations.description: For enum renames, use codebase-wide grep to confirm no dangling references in tests, docs, or related modules
New name 'Completions' aligns with verb-noun pattern (GenerateCompletion → Completions noun form) - consistent with command intent
Ensure CLI integration tests exercise this command path to validate the rename didn't break functionality
If there's user-facing CLI documentation mentioning 'generate-completion', update to match new command name exposure
